name: Mock Test - 03
instruction: The Question Paper contains three sections. Section A has 25 questions. Attempt any 20 questions. Section B has 24 questions. Attempt any 20 questions. Section C has 6 questions. Attempt any 5 questions. All questions carry equal marks. There is no negative marking.

questions.question_text: What type of interaction hold the molecules together in a polar molecular solid?
questions.question_html: What type of interaction hold the molecules together in a polar molecular solid?
questions.explanation: Polar molecule will act as a dipole.

questions.question_text: Benzoic acid dissolved in benzene shows a molecular weight of:
questions.question_html: Benzoic acid dissolved in benzene shows a molecular weight of:

questions.question_text: Hydrogen sulphide reacts with lead acetate forming a black compound that reacts with hydrogen peroxide to form another compound. The colour of the compound is:
questions.question_html: Hydrogen sulphide reacts with lead acetate forming a black compound that reacts with hydrogen peroxide to form another compound. The colour of the compound is:
questions.explanation: {tex}{\left( {C{H_3}COO} \right)_2}Pb + \mathop {{H_2}S \to PbS\; \downarrow }\limits_{Black\;ppt} + 2C{H_3}COOH{/tex} {tex}4{H_2}{O_2} + PbS \to \mathop {PbS{O_4}\; \downarrow }\limits_{White\;ppt} + 4{H_2}O{/tex}

questions.question_text: Freon can:
questions.question_html: Freon can:

options.text: both initiate radical chain reactions in the stratosphere and disturb natural ozone balance.
options.html: both initiate radical chain reactions in the stratosphere and disturb natural ozone balance.

options.text: initiate radical chain reactions in the stratosphere.
options.html: initiate radical chain reactions in the stratosphere.

questions.correct_option_text: both initiate radical chain reactions in the stratosphere and disturb natural ozone balance.
questions.explanation: Most freon, even that used in refrigeration, eventually makes its way into the atmosphere where it diffuses unchanged into the stratosphere. In the stratosphere, freon is able to initiate radical chain reactions that can upset the natural ozone balance.

questions.question_html: By heating phenol with chloroform in alkali, it is converted into
questions.explanation: Salicylaldehyde

questions.question_text: The monomeric unis of starch is / are :
questions.question_html: The monomeric unis of starch is / are :
questions.explanation: Starch is a polymer of α-glucose.

questions.question_text: Which among the following is not an example of amorphous solids?
questions.question_html: Which among the following is not an example of amorphous solids?
questions.explanation: Quartz is not amorphous solids because unlike amorphous solids it has long range order. Quartz is a crystalline solid.

questions.question_text: Example of solution of liquid in gas is
questions.question_html: Example of solution of liquid in gas is
questions.explanation: Solute is water (liquid) and solvent is gas( air).

questions.question_text: When chlorine is passed through a concentrated hot solution of KOH, the compound formed is_____.
questions.question_html: When chlorine is passed through a concentrated hot solution of KOH, the compound formed is_____.
questions.explanation: Cl 2 on treatment with conc. Base form ClO 3 - ion. Cl 2 + 6KOH {tex}\to{/tex} 5KCl + KClO 3 + 3H 2 O

questions.question_text: Vinylic halides contain:
questions.question_html: Vinylic halides contain:

options.text: a halogen atom bonded to an sp 2 -hybridized carbon atom of a carbon-carbon double bond.
options.html: a halogen atom bonded to an sp<sup>2</sup>-hybridized carbon atom of a carbon-carbon double bond.

options.text: a sp 3 -hybridized carbon atom, next to an aromatic ring, to which halogen atom is bonded.
options.html: a sp<sup>3</sup>-hybridized carbon atom, next to an aromatic ring, to which halogen atom is bonded.

options.text: a sp 2 -hybridized carbon atom of an aromatic ring which is bonded to a halogen.
options.html: a sp<sup>2</sup>-hybridized carbon atom of an aromatic ring which is bonded to a halogen.

options.text: a halogen atom bonded to an sp 3 -hybridized carbon atom next to carbon-carbon double bond.
options.html: a halogen atom bonded to an sp<sup>3</sup>-hybridized carbon atom next to carbon-carbon double bond.

questions.correct_option_text: a halogen atom bonded to an sp 2 -hybridized carbon atom of a carbon-carbon double bond.
questions.explanation: Vinylic halides are the compounds in which the halogen atom is bonded to an sp 2 -hybridized carbon atom of a carbon-carbon double bond (C = C). For example CH 2 =CHBr.

questions.question_text: Arrange the following compounds in the increasing order of their densities.
questions.question_html: Arrange the following compounds in the increasing order of their densities.
questions.explanation: Alkyl halides are heavier in density than water. Their densities are based on the masses of the halogen atoms and the number of halogen atoms, and also the carbon atoms. Simply putting, the atomic mass of Br is 79u and that of Cl is 35u. From the molecules, (d) will have the heaviest mass as it contains 2 Br, followed by (c), (b), and (a). Density is directly proportional to mass, hence the order will be the same in terms of reducing densities.

questions.question_text: Acetylation of glucose yields
questions.question_html: Acetylation of glucose yields
questions.explanation: Acetylation of glucose with acetic anhydride gives glucose pentaacetate which confirms the presence of five –OH groups. Since it exists as a stable compound, five –OH groups should be attached to different carbon atoms.

questions.question_text: The unit cell of a substance has cations A+ at the corners of the unit cell and the anions B - in the center. The simplest formula of the substance is
questions.question_html: The unit cell of a substance has cations A+ at the corners of the unit cell and the anions B- in the center. The simplest formula of the substance is
questions.explanation: Contribution of A {tex}=8 \times \frac{1}{8}{\text{ }} = {\text{ }}1{/tex} Contribution of B = 1. Therefore, Formula is AB.

questions.question_text: Which of the following types of compounds will have Vant Hoff factor = 1?
questions.question_html: Which of the following types of compounds will have Vant Hoff factor = 1?
questions.explanation: Since urea is a non-electrolyte, Hence, the Van't Hoff factor is equal to 1.

questions.question_text: Which of the following elements can be involved in p {tex}\pi{/tex} –d {tex}\pi{/tex} bonding?
questions.question_html: Which of the following elements can be involved in p{tex}\pi{/tex}–d{tex}\pi{/tex} bonding?
questions.explanation: Phosphorus can be involved in p {tex}\pi{/tex} - d {tex}\pi{/tex} bonding due to the presence of vacant d orbitals Carbon, Nitrogen, and Boron does not have d orbitals.

questions.question_html: Night blindness is caused by the deficiency of

questions.question_html: Which crystal system has axial angles equal to 90o?
questions.explanation: The four orthorhombic lattices have unequal sides, angle between all faces is 90°.

questions.question_text: At a given temperature, the osmotic pressure of a concentrated solution of a substance ________.
questions.question_html: At a given temperature, the osmotic pressure of a concentrated solution of a substance ________.

options.text: is higher than that at a dilute solution.
options.html: is higher than that at a dilute solution.

options.text: is same as that of a dilute solution.
options.html: is same as that of a dilute solution.

options.text: cannot be compared with the osmotic pressure of the dilute solution.
options.html: cannot be compared with the osmotic pressure of the dilute solution.

options.text: is lower than that of a dilute solution.
options.html: is lower than that of a dilute solution.

questions.correct_option_text: is higher than that at a dilute solution.
questions.explanation: {tex}\pi{/tex} = CRT where C is the concentration of the solution and pi is the osmotic pressure. So, the higher the concentration of solution at a given temperature the higher will be the osmotic pressure

questions.question_text: When KBr is treated with conc. H 2 SO 4 , reddish brown gas is evolved. The gas is:
questions.question_html: When KBr is treated with conc. H2SO4, reddish brown gas is evolved. The gas is:
questions.explanation: Br – get oxidized to Br 2 on treatment with H 2 SO 4. 2KBr + 2H 2 SO 4 {tex}\to{/tex} K 2 SO 4 + SO 2 {tex}\uparrow{/tex} + Br 2 {tex}\uparrow{/tex} + 2H 2 O. Concentrated sulphuric acid oxidizes HBr to Bromine.

questions.question_text: IUPAC name of (CH 3 ) 3 CCl is:
questions.question_html: IUPAC name of (CH3)3CCl is:
questions.explanation: Longest chain will be of three carbon to which Cl and CH 3 will be attached at 2 positions.

questions.question_text: Which of the following is most acidic?
questions.question_html: Which of the following is most acidic?
questions.explanation: In cases of halogen derivatives of phenols or aniline or benzoic acid etc, it is very helpful to understand that all halogens, when attached to benzene ring, exerts -I as well as +R effect. In case of Cl, Br and I, the +R effect has almost no effect on reactivity, acidic character or basic character of the benzene ring. It is due to very less effective overlapping involving 2p of carbon and 3p or 4p or 5p of halogen. Hence, only -I effect becomes the deciding factor, which is most dominant from ortho-position and least effective from para-position. So m chlorophenol is most acidic.

questions.question_text: One or more of the following vitamin is insoluble in water
questions.question_html: One or more of the following vitamin is insoluble in water
questions.explanation: Vitamins which are soluble in fat and oils but insoluble in water are kept in fat soluble vitamins. These are vitamins A, D, E and K. They are stored in liver and adipose (fat storing) tissues. All these are insoluble in water.

ionic solid with [PCl 4 ] + tetrahedral and [PCl 6 ] - octahedral

questions.explanation: Structure of PCl 5 in solid state The cation, [PCl 4 ] + The anion, [PCl 6 ] -

questions.question_html: Which transition metal oxide has appearance and conductivity like that of copper?
questions.explanation: Rhenium oxide and copper oxide have same appearance and conductivity.

questions.question_html: Aquatic animals are more comfortable in cold water rather that warm water because

options.html: Solubility of gas in liquid increases with decrease in temperature

options.text: Solubility of gas in liquid changes with pressure
options.html: Solubility of gas in liquid changes with pressure

options.text: Concentration increases with increase in temperature
options.html: Concentration increases with increase in temperature

questions.correct_option_text: Solubility of gas in liquid increases with decrease in temperature
questions.explanation: Because of increase in kinetic energy of gas molecules with temperature there tendency to escape from the liquid will increase.

questions.question_text: Which of the following is most soluble in water?
questions.question_html: Which of the following is most soluble in water?
questions.explanation: Amino acids are water-soluble, high melting solids and behave like salts rather than simple amines or carboxylic acids. This behaviour is due to the presence of both acidic (carboxyl group) and basic (amino group) groups in the same molecule. In aqueous solution, the carboxyl group can lose a proton and amino group can accept a proton, giving rise to a dipolar ion known as zwitter ion. This is neutral but contains both positive and negative charges. Because of the resulting polar interactions in water they are soluble in water.

questions.question_text: Which of the following elements does not show allotropy?
questions.question_html: Which of the following elements does not show allotropy?
questions.explanation: Nitrogen does not show allotropy due to its weak N-N single bond. Therefore, the ability of the nitrogen to form a polymeric structure or more than one structure becomes less. Therefore, the ability of nitrogen to form a polymeric structure is less.

questions.question_text: Which is the correct IUPAC name for {tex}C{{H}_{3}}-\underset{{{C}_{2}}{{H}_{5}}}{\mathop{\underset{|}{\mathop{CH}}\,}}\,-C{{H}_{2}}-Br{/tex} ?
questions.question_html: Which is the correct IUPAC name for {tex}C{{H}_{3}}-\underset{{{C}_{2}}{{H}_{5}}}{\mathop{\underset{|}{\mathop{CH}}\,}}\,-C{{H}_{2}}-Br{/tex}?
questions.explanation: First, we need to identify the longest carbon chain. Once we do that, the actual structure should read CH 3 —CH 2 —CH(CH 3 )—CH 2 —Br. –Br, the functional halide group is attached to the first carbon atom (1- Bromo), so we start the numbering from that position. The methyl group branch is bond to the second carbon atom in the chain(2-methyl). The number of carbons in the unbranched parent chain is four, thus giving the name butane. The IUPAC is named 1-Bromo-2-methylbutane.

questions.question_text: Which gas is evolved when the urea is treated with NaOH?
questions.question_html: Which gas is evolved when the urea is treated with NaOH?
questions.explanation: H 2 NCONH 2 + 2NaOH {tex}\to{/tex} 2NH 3 + Na 2 CO 3

questions.question_text: Certain vitamins cannot be stored in the body because
questions.question_html: Certain vitamins cannot be stored in the body because

options.text: they are readily excreted in urine
options.html: they are readily excreted in urine

options.text: they are used up very fast in the body
options.html: they are used up very fast in the body

questions.correct_option_text: they are readily excreted in urine
questions.explanation: B group vitamins and vitamin C are soluble in water so they are grouped together as water soluble vitamins. These must be supplied regularly in diet because they are readily excreted in urine and cannot be stored (except vitamin B 12 ) in our body.

questions.question_html: The most unlikely representation of resonance structures of p-nitrophenoxide ion is:

questions.question_text: At equilibrium, the rate of dissolution of a solid solute in a volatile liquid solvent is ________.
questions.question_html: At equilibrium, the rate of dissolution of a solid solute in a volatile liquid solvent is ________.

options.text: equal to the rate of crystallisation
options.html: equal to the rate of crystallisation

options.text: greater than the rate of crystallisation
options.html: greater than the rate of crystallisation

options.text: less than the rate of crystallisation
options.html: less than the rate of crystallisation

questions.correct_option_text: equal to the rate of crystallisation
questions.explanation: At equilibrium, the rate of dissolution of solid solute is equal to the rate of crystallisation. As the number of solute particles going into the solution will equal to the solute particle separating out.

questions.question_text: Which of the following is thermally the most stable?
questions.question_html: Which of the following is thermally the most stable?
questions.explanation: The stability of hydrides decreases down the group so the most stable is H 2 O. The thermal stability decreases as the atomic mass increases. Water dissociates at 2000 0 C while tellurium hydride, H 2 Te decomposes at room temperature. This is due to an increase in the bond length of M-H (M- O, S, Se, Te). Thus the thermal stability decreases as the atomic size increases. As with the increase in atomic size, the bond length also increases which decreases the thermal stability.

questions.question_text: Arrange the following in the increasing order of nucleophilicity: I - , Cl - , Br -
questions.question_html:
Arrange the following in the increasing order of nucleophilicity:
I-, Cl-, Br-
questions.explanation: Nucleophilicity means the tendency of a nucleophile to attack a center of a positive charge. As the size of the nucleophile increases, its basicity decreases, and hence its nucleophilicity increases. As we move down the group 17 size of the anions increases and thus the nucleophilicity increases as Cl - <Br - <I -

questions.question_text: Packing efficiency in body centered cubic structure is
questions.question_html: Packing efficiency in body centered cubic structure is
questions.explanation: Packing efficiency {tex}= \frac{{{\text{Volume occupied by four sheres in the unit cell}} \times {\text{100}}}}{{{\text{Total volume of the unit cell}}}}\%{/tex} {tex} = \frac{{4 \times \left( {\frac{4}{3}} \right)\pi {r^3} \times 100}}{{{{\left[ {\left( {\frac{4}{{\sqrt 3 }}} \right)r} \right]}^3}}}\% {/tex} {tex}= \frac{{\left( {\frac{8}{3}} \right)\pi {r^3} \times 100}}{{\frac{{64}}{{\left( {3\sqrt 3 } \right){r^3}}}}}\% = 68\%{/tex}

questions.explanation: CH 3 MgBr reacts with CH 3 OH and form CH 4 . Grignard Reagent act as both base as well as a nucleophile. In the presence of alcohol, H 2 O or other groups having acidic hydrogen Grignard reagent act as base and abstract acidic H.

questions.question_text: When a colourless gas is passed through the bromine water, it decolorizes. The gas is:
questions.question_html: When a colourless gas is passed through the bromine water, it decolorizes. The gas is:
questions.explanation: SO 2 . It is because, bromine water, being a good oxidizing agent oxidizes SO 2 to H 2 SO 4. SO 2 + Br 2 + 2H 2 O {tex}\rightarrow{/tex} 2HBr + H 2 SO 4

questions.question_text: Assertion (A): Semiconductors are solids with conductivities in the intermediate range from 10 –6 – 10 4 ohm –1 m –1 . Reason (R): Intermediate conductivity in a semiconductor is due to the partially filled valence band.
questions.question_html:
Assertion (A): Semiconductors are solids with conductivities in the intermediate range from 10–6 – 104 ohm–1m–1.
Reason (R): Intermediate conductivity in a semiconductor is due to the partially filled valence band.

options.text: A is true but R is false.
options.html: A is true but R is false.

options.text: A is false but R is true.
options.html: A is false but R is true.

options.html: Both A and R are true but R is not the correct explanation of A.

questions.correct_option_text: A is true but R is false.
questions.explanation: The conductivities of semiconductors lies in between the conductors and insulators and of range 10 –6 – 10 4 ohm –1 m –1 . But intermediate conductivity is due to the small energy gap between valance and conduction band which is zero in case of conductors and very large in case of insulators. It has a filled valance band. So, A is true but R is false.

questions.question_text: On the basis of information given below mark the correct option. Information: In bromoethane and chloroethane mixture intermolecular interactions of A-A and B-B type are nearly the same as A-B type interactions. In ethanol and acetone mixture A-A or B-B type, intermolecular interactions are stronger than A-B type interactions. In chloroform and acetone mixture A-A or B-B type, intermolecular interactions are weaker than A-B type interactions.
questions.question_html:
On the basis of information given below mark the correct option.
Information:

  1. In bromoethane and chloroethane mixture intermolecular interactions of A-A and B-B type are nearly the same as A-B type interactions.
  2. In ethanol and acetone mixture A-A or B-B type, intermolecular interactions are stronger than A-B type interactions.
  3. In chloroform and acetone mixture A-A or B-B type, intermolecular interactions are weaker than A-B type interactions.

questions.explanation: Solution (A) will follow Raoult’s law. For an ideal solution to follow Raoult's law A-A and B-B intermolecular interactions should be nearly the same as A-B type interactions.

options.text: They can donate a proton to a stronger base
options.html: They can donate a proton to a stronger base

questions.explanation: Phenol reacts with sodium hydroxide solution to give a colourless solution containing sodium phenoxide. In this reaction, the hydrogen ion has been removed by the strongly basic hydroxide ion in the sodium hydroxide solution.

questions.question_text: Instructions for Question 53 to 55: Ozone is an unstable, dark blue diamagnetic gas. It absorbs the UV radiation strongly, thus protecting the people on earth from the harmful UV-radiation from the sun. The use of chlorofluorocarbon (CFC) in aerosol and refrigerators and their subsequent escape into the atmosphere, is blamed for making holes in the ozone layer over the Antarctica. Ozone acts as a strong oxidising agent in acidic and alkaline medium. For this property, ozone is used as a germicide and disinfectant for sterilizing water. It is also used in laboratory for the ozonolysis of organic compounds and in industry for the manufacture of potassium permanganate, artificial silk, etc. Which of the following statements is not correct for ozone?
questions.question_html:
Instructions for Question 53 to 55:

Ozone is an unstable, dark blue diamagnetic gas. It absorbs the UV radiation strongly, thus protecting the people on earth from the harmful UV-radiation from the sun. The use of chlorofluorocarbon (CFC) in aerosol and refrigerators and their subsequent escape into the atmosphere, is blamed for making holes in the ozone layer over the Antarctica. Ozone acts as a strong oxidising agent in acidic and alkaline medium. For this property, ozone is used as a germicide and disinfectant for sterilizing water. It is also used in laboratory for the ozonolysis of organic compounds and in industry for the manufacture of potassium permanganate, artificial silk, etc.


Which of the following statements is not correct for ozone?

options.text: It cannot act as bleaching agent in dry state.
options.html: It cannot act as bleaching agent in dry state.

questions.correct_option_text: It cannot act as bleaching agent in dry state.
questions.explanation: It cannot act as bleaching agent in dry state.

questions.question_html:
Instructions for Question 53 to 55:

Ozone is an unstable, dark blue diamagnetic gas. It absorbs the UV radiation strongly, thus protecting the people on earth from the harmful UV-radiation from the sun. The use of chlorofluorocarbon (CFC) in aerosol and refrigerators and their subsequent escape into the atmosphere, is blamed for making holes in the ozone layer over the Antarctica. Ozone acts as a strong oxidising agent in acidic and alkaline medium. For this property, ozone is used as a germicide and disinfectant for sterilizing water. Ozone gives carbonyl compounds with

options.text: alkenes followed by decomposition with Zn/H 2 O
options.html: alkenes followed by decomposition with Zn/H<sub>2</sub>O

options.text: alcohols followed by decomposition with Zn/H 2 O.
options.html: alcohols followed by decomposition with Zn/H<sub>2</sub>O.

questions.correct_option_text: alkenes followed by decomposition with Zn/H 2 O
questions.explanation: Ozone reacts with alkenes to form ozonides which on hydrolysis or reduction gives carbonyl compounds.

questions.explanation: I 2 + 5O 3 + H 2 O {tex}\to{/tex} 2HIO 3 + 5O 2
